CONTENT: 1️⃣ Before the Review
Confirm Sprint Goal is clearly defined and documented
Verify all demo items meet the Definition of Done
Prepare demo scenarios focused on user journeys and business outcomes
Invite stakeholders and share the agenda
Set up the demo environment and confirm access
2️⃣ Welcome & Context (5 mins)
Restate the Sprint Goal
Summarise what was planned vs what was completed
Set expectations — this is an interactive session
3️⃣ Increment Demo (20–40 mins)
Demonstrate only completed work that meets the Definition of Done
Walk through user journeys rather than ticket-by-ticket
Highlight business outcomes and value delivered
Invite questions and observations throughout
4️⃣ Stakeholder Feedback (15–30 mins)
Open the floor for general discussion
Capture what is working well
Capture gaps and missing functionality
Capture suggested improvements and new ideas
Confirm feedback has been noted and will be actioned
5️⃣ Product Backlog Review (10–20 mins)
Review the current state of the product backlog
Adjust priorities based on stakeholder feedback
Add or refine backlog items live where possible
Confirm Product Owner owns all backlog updates
6️⃣ Metrics & Progress (Optional)
Review velocity or throughput for the sprint
Summarise progress toward the release goal
7️⃣ Wrap-Up (5 mins)
Confirm next steps and owners
Summarise key decisions made during the review
Remind the team of the next Sprint Review date
8️⃣ After the Review
Convert all captured feedback into backlog items
Update backlog priorities to reflect latest insights
Document key decisions and share with stakeholders
Share recording or session notes if applicable
